# v0.4.1 — Release tooling fix: no more auto-generated bump commits

**Status:** shipped, tagged `v0.4.1`.

## Goal

`make release` used to always create its own `release: vX.Y.Z` commit
(bumping `VERSION`) and tag *that*, one commit downstream of whatever
feature work actually preceded it. That meant the tagged commit's own
message was always generic — visible directly in GitHub Actions' run
list, which is what prompted this: a `v0.3.0` run titled with the real
feature commit's message (because that tag was created by hand, directly
on the feature commit, bypassing `make release`) sitting next to a
`v0.4.0` run titled just `release: v0.4.0`.

## Delivered scope

- **`make release` no longer creates any commit.** It now: bumps
  `VERSION` (rewrites the file — the release pipeline itself doesn't read
  it; both `release.yml` builds version everything from
  `github.ref_name`, the git tag, not the file, so this is purely local
  convenience for `make version`/`make docker-build`/the help banner),
  tags whatever commit is currently at `HEAD`, and pushes the tag. That's
  it — no `git add`, no `git commit`, no `git push origin HEAD`.
- **Practical effect**: as long as you commit your feature work with a
  real message *before* running `make release`, the tag lands directly on
  that commit — GitHub Actions' run list shows your actual message, not a
  bump commit's, automatically, every time. No need to tag by hand to get
  that.
- **`VERSION` ends up rewritten-but-uncommitted** after `make release`
  runs — intentional. Commit it yourself whenever convenient (fold it
  into your next commit, or its own); the release itself doesn't wait on
  or depend on that commit existing.
- **You're responsible for `git push origin main`** (or whatever your
  default branch is) — `make release` only pushes the tag. Skipping this
  means the tag's commit is pushed (tags carry their target commit) but
  isn't visible as part of `main`'s history on GitHub until you push the
  branch too.
- **`make tag` removed entirely.** It only ever did `git tag` + `git push
  origin <tag>` off `VERSION_CURRENT` — fully redundant with (and strictly
  worse than) `release`, which does the same tag+push plus the `VERSION`
  bump, in one command. Nothing else referenced it except a stray
  `CLAUDE.md` line in `servicedesk` (fixed).
- Same fixes applied to `~/code/servicedesk`'s `Makefile` for consistency
  — not part of this repo, no release note here, just noted for context.

## Bugs found and fixed

- Iterated through four designs before landing here, each one a genuine
  correction from feedback, not a false start caught in review:
  1. Bump + commit + tag + push HEAD (original) — tagged commit always
     generic.
  2. Require `VERSION` file to already match, fail otherwise, push tag
     only — removed the auto-commit, but added friction (a separate
     `make bump` + manual commit step before every release) and still
     pushed `HEAD`.
  3. Same as above, minus pushing `HEAD` — closer, but still required the
     separate `make bump` step as a precondition.
  4. **Shipped**: `release` calls `bump` inline (no separate step
     required), never commits, never pushes `HEAD` — simplest of the
     four, and the one that actually satisfies "I'll handle git
     add/commit, `make release` should just tag and push the tag."
     `make tag` removed as redundant once `release` covered its exact job
     and more.

## Verified

`make -n release VERSION=x.y.z` dry-run reviewed for both grepod and
servicedesk; `make release VERSION=<bogus>` / matching-version paths
exercised for real (grepod only — no actual tag/push in either case,
confirmed via `git tag -l`/`git ls-remote --tags origin` showing no stray
refs); `make help` banner output reviewed.
